aas:langStringTextType.aas:text: This model describes the properties that are necessary to communicate zero kilometer failures of products from the customer to the supplier. Zero km failures are issues with a vehicle component that occur during vehicle assembly.
Describes and identifies the given part which is suspected to cause the zero kilometre failure.
A local identifier enables identification of a part in a specific dataspace, but is not unique in the Catena-X dataspace. Multiple local identifiers may exist.
A local identifier enables identification of a part in a specific dataspace, but is not unique in the Catena-X dataspace. Multiple local identifiers may exist.
Describes and identifies the vehicle or subassambly in which the part has been assemblied into.
Information on the plant at which the failure has been discovered (during assembly) for a given part.
Amount of defect parts per shift
Description of the defect as human readable text.
A list of diagnostic trouble codes.
A list of diagnostic trouble codes.
A list of environment conditions: E.g. outside temperature measured by the vehicle, a specific value measured by on ECU, ....
A list of environment conditions: E.g. outside temperature measured by the vehicle, a specific value measured by on ECU, ....
List of references to related items, e.g. quality tasks. The references are unique within this data space.

This model describes the properties that are necessary to communicate zero kilometer failures of products from the customer to the supplier. Zero km failures are issues with a vehicle component that occur during vehicle assembly.

aas:langStringDefinitionTypeIec61360.aas:text: This model describes the properties that are necessary to communicate zero kilometer failures of products from the customer to the supplier. Zero km failures are issues with a vehicle component that occur during vehicle assembly.

String that is encoded in a data matrix code. Data matrix codes or short DMC are labelled/printed on parts. Example: A data matrix code can combine supplier name, part type, manufacturing and many more information. A DMC string is often unique within this data provider.
Name of the part as assigned by the manufacturer
The key of a local identifier.
The value of an identifier.
A local identifier enables identification of a part in a specific dataspace, but is not unique in the Catena-X dataspace. Multiple local identifiers may exist.

Part ID as assigned by the customer of the part. The customer Part ID identifies the part (as designed)in the customer`s dataspace. The Part ID does not reference a specific instance of a part and thus should not be confused with the serial number or any other instance IDs. If no specific part ID exists a part family ID may be substituted for it.
Describes and identifies the given part which is suspected to cause the zero kilometre failure.
Vehicle manufacturer-specific anonymized VIN, which is still a unique identifier within this data provider.
Vehicle manufacturer-specific model identifier or Vehicle manufacturer-specific project name.
Vehicle manufacturer-specific identifier/type of the installed engine
Describes and identifies the vehicle or subassambly in which the part has been assemblied into.
Timestamp of the assembly of the given part.
The name of the assembly line in the plant, where the given part has been assemblied into the vehicle.
The quantity value associated with the unit.
The unit of a countable attribute.
Amount of defect parts per shift
Identifier of the plant site that uniqly identifies the plant within this dataspace.
Information on the plant at which the failure has been discovered (during assembly) for a given part.
Description of the defect as human readable text.
Hex value of this DTC

A human-readable name that is the result of the translation of DTC hex code. There is a 1:1 relationship between DTC hex code and DTC full name. DTC full name must include the fault byte added after the minus. DTC full name is unique: That means that at one occurrence timestamp and the same vehicle and the same ECU only one DTC full name can be set.
Description of this DTC. If description name consists of main DTC and failure byte, than both description strings are concatenated using a "-" as separator.
OEM-specific: Fault path for this DTC. Allows further analysis
OEM-specific description of DTC fault path

freeze frame from ecu. The freeze frame records many parameters of the DTC and surrounding parameters like outside temperature when the DTC was set. It is a very long HEX string with many OEM-specific and ECU-specific content in
Describes whether this DTC sets the MIL (malfunction indicator light) in the dashboard.
Counter how often this DTC was set in total
mileage in km when the DTC occurred the first time
Unit of occurrence mileage - either km or mi.
date and time when the DTC occurred the first time/was recorded the first time in the ECU
OEM-specific state of DTC: 0;1 (permanent/temporary/intermediate), could also be a string with permanent, temporary, intermediate, ....
Indicator whether this DTC was stored as Error or Info
OEM-specific: Primary key for this condition consists of unique identifier of env. condition and DTC
Date and time when this condition/information was created.
The description of the environment condition/information
Hex value of the condition.
The numeric value of the condition as double.
Text value of the condition.
The unit of measurement for the environment condition value.
A list of environment conditions: E.g. outside temperature measured by the vehicle, a specific value measured by on ECU, ....
A list of diagnostic trouble codes.
List of references to related items, e.g. quality tasks. The references are unique within this data space.
Defect related data
